mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5% and inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains elevated above 320, the Fed maintains restrictive monetary policy. This sustained high real rates compress equity valuations and push 10-year Treasury yields upward as market participants price in prolonged rate persistence. The mechanism operates continuously in this regime without additional triggers.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 4.33% and CPIAUCSL at 320.321 indicate persistent monetary restriction
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with sticky inflation
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines in more than 40% of 20-day forward windows when FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and CPIAUCSL > 320, or Rank IC < 0.05 over rolling 2-year periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) above 4.5% signals labor market deterioration and recession risk. This triggers a flight-to-safety bid in Treasury securities, driving 10-year yields downward as investors reduce equity exposure and rotate into fixed income. The mechanism activates only when unemployment crosses the threshold.
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE at 4.2% is near threshold
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE > 4.5%, or the correlation between UNRATE changes and yield changes is positive (r > 0.1) over any 12-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly signal consumer demand strength. When both series decline month-over-month by more than 2%, aggregate demand weakens, reducing inflation expectations and causing 10-year Treasury yields to fall. This interaction-driven mechanism requires simultaneous weakness in both demand proxies to activate.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline >2% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 724131.0M and HOUST at 1324.0K require baseline comparison for decline detection
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in more than 45% of triggered instances, or the joint signal (RSAFS AND HOUST decline) produces lower predictive power than either series alone (Rank IC difference < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth above 104.5 indicates robust supply-side expansion and productivity gains. This reduces unit labor costs and inflation pressures, causing 10-year Treasury yields to decline as the market reprices inflation expectations downward. The mechanism operates as a continuous trend signal without regime switching.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 103.8799 is below 104.5 threshold, suggesting supply constraint
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in more than 50% of 60-day forward windows when INDPRO > 104.5, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates below 100k new jobs per month, signaling labor market cooling. This weakens aggregate income and consumption, reducing inflation expectations and causing 10-year Treasury yields to fall as the market prices in lower future growth and potential Fed rate cuts.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly change falls below 100k jobs
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 159517K requires month-over-month change calculation
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in more than 55% of instances when PAYEMS growth < 100k, or the correlation between PAYEMS changes and yield changes is positive (r > 0.15) over any 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) accelerates above 3% annualized, signaling strong economic expansion. This increases inflation expectations and reduces the real yield floor, causing 10-year Treasury yields to rise as investors demand higher nominal compensation. The mechanism is regime-conditional: it operates only during expansion phases, not recessions.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 23526.085B requires quarterly growth calculation
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines in more than 50% of 60-day forward windows during periods of GDPC1 growth > 3%, or Rank IC < 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction between elevated federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS > 4.25%) and declining inflation (CPIAUCSL month-over-month change < 0.2%) signals real rate tightening. This combination reduces equity risk appetite and increases Treasury demand, causing 10-year yields to fall as investors flee to safety and the Fed faces pressure to cut rates.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 4.33% meets first condition
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction signal produces lower predictive power than FEDFUNDS alone (Rank IC difference < 0.03).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline below 1300k units annualized, indicating residential construction weakness. This signals weakening demand for materials, labor, and credit, reducing inflation expectations and causing 10-year Treasury yields to fall as the market reprices growth and inflation downward.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in more than 55% of instances when HOUST < 1300k, or the correlation between HOUST changes and yield changes is positive (r > 0.12) over any 12-month window.
